title: Role validation warnings for cross-agent-type field misuse
description: When a role has agent_type: codex but uses Claude-specific fields (claude_config_dir, hooks, settings, permission_mode), emit a warning. Similarly if a Claude role uses codex-specific fields. Helps users catch config mistakes.

comments.author: misc-fog
comments.text:
CODE REVIEW (misc-fog) of coder-2-fog/h2-mzc-role-warnings @ 2e46c4a — vs main.

VERDICT: Approve with one recommended fix (P2). Core design is correct and well-tested; the one gap is catalog completeness.

WHAT'S SOLID:
- Trigger logic correct in both directions (f.owner != active && f.isSet); no false positives across the matrix. Verified all 13 unit tests pass + go vet clean on internal/config + internal/cmd.
- stdout/stderr separation is clean: emitRoleWarnings → os.Stderr; printDryRun → stdout (and dry-run output is human text, not JSON). No output-path corruption.
- Ordering is safe: Role.Validate() runs at load time (role.go:612), so by the time emitRoleWarnings runs the active harness is always a valid ValidHarnessTypes value or empty→claude_code default. No 'unknown harness warns everything' path.
- All launch paths covered exactly once: run/bridge/pod → doSetupAndForkAgent → emitRoleWarnings; dry-run calls it directly then returns before setupAndForkAgent (no double-warn). Runs after ApplyOverrides so it reflects effective config.
- Field catalog is complete on the codex side and for claude config/permission/hooks/settings.

P2 FINDING (false negative — recommend fixing before merge; trivial):
'system_prompt' is missing from harnessSpecificFields. It's a user-settable role field documented as Claude's --system-prompt (role.go:284) and is consumed ONLY by the claude harness (claude/harness.go:89); the codex harness reads Instructions only and never SystemPrompt. So a codex/generic role that sets system_prompt has it silently ignored — exactly the misuse class this feature exists to flag. Empirically verified: a codex role with SystemPrompt set → 0 warnings (control field claude_permission_mode → 1 warning).
FIX: add {"system_prompt", "claude_code", func(r *Role) bool { return r.SystemPrompt != "" }} to the catalog + a test mirroring TestWarnings_CodexHarnessWithClaudePermissionMode.

P3 NIT (optional, acceptable as-is): yamlNodeSet treats a present-but-null 'hooks:'/'settings:' (null scalar, Kind!=0) as set, so it would warn on an effectively-empty key. The realistic absent-key case decodes to the zero Node and is handled correctly; the null-present case is defensible (user did mention the key). No change required.

Recommend: land after adding the system_prompt catalog entry + test. Everything else is merge-ready.
